paper_title: Palynological analysis of a late Holocene core from Santo Antônio da Patrulha, Rio Grande do Sul, Southern Brazil

methods.description: Process for extracting and storing peat bog core samples for further analysis.

steps.description: The core was taken from the deepest portion near the center of the peat bog using a Russian corer sampler, whose maximum depth reached 115 cm.
steps.searchable_summary: extract core sample from peat bog using Russian corer

steps.description: Sections of 50 cm length were extruded in situ, wrapped in plastic film and aluminum foils.
steps.searchable_summary: extrude and wrap core sections in plastic and foil

steps.description: The section was transported to the Laboratory and stored in special conditions (ca.+4°C) before sampling.
steps.searchable_summary: transport and store core sections at 4°C

methods.description: Dating of sediment subsamples using Accelerator Mass Spectrometry (AMS) and calibration of radiocarbon datings.

steps.description: Three sediment subsamples of 3 cm thickness were taken from the core and dated by the Accelerator Mass Spectrometry (AMS) in the CAIS Laboratory of the University of Georgia (USA).
steps.searchable_summary: perform AMS radiocarbon dating on sediment subsamples

steps.description: The calibration of radiocarbon datings was carried out after CALPAL (Weninger et al. 2004).

methods.description: Analysis of pollen and charcoal from subsamples to determine concentration and accumulation rates.

steps.description: 35 subsamples (1 cm³ volume) were taken at 3 cm intervals along the 115 cm core.
steps.searchable_summary: take subsamples at 3 cm intervals from core

steps.description: All subsamples were processed by standard pollen analytical methods using HF, HCl, KOH, acetolysis, followed by filtering through a 250 μm net.
steps.searchable_summary: process subsamples using standard pollen methods

steps.description: Pollen preparation included the addition of exotic Lycopodium clavatum L. spores to determine pollen concentration and accumulation rates.
steps.searchable_summary: add Lycopodium spores for concentration determination

steps.description: A minimum of 300 pollen grains were counted for each subsample.
steps.searchable_summary: count pollen grains in each subsample

steps.description: Calculations of concentration were also made for the carbonized particles.

methods.description: Visualization and analysis of pollen data using software tools for plotting and cluster analysis.

steps.description: Softwares TILIA, TGVIEW and CONISS version 2.0.2 were used for plotting the pollen data, calculations and cluster analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: plot pollen data and perform cluster analysis using TILIA, TGVIEW, and CONISS

steps.description: The phases of the pollen record were established from changes in the pollen assemblages and from cluster analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: establish pollen record phases from assemblages and cluster analysis

methods.description: Ordination by Principal Coordinates Analysis (PCoA) for a synthetic view of community compositional changes.

steps.description: PCoA was based on pairwise Euclidean distances among subsamples.
The significance of ordination axes was evaluated by bootstrap.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate ordination axes significance using bootstrap
